topic: In China employee termination: scenario-led cross-border control guide for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination scenarios foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal basis used to evaluate scenario route choices and consequence logic.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports scenario-level implementation interpretation and practical constraints.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds dispute-facing lens for scenario risk evaluation and evidence sensitivity.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors scenario offboarding obligations linked to contribution closure.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical foreign-employer scenario framing and implementation cautions.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from troubleshooting format to scenario-led format while keeping legal-source hierarchy stable.
Excluded lower-fit generalized comparative and tax-centric materials for this legal-scenario focus.
Retained China Briefing as practical translator for multinational readers.
consensus: Scenario-led writing improves foreign-reader comprehension when legal context precedes operational action.
Most risk escalation in scenarios comes from route ambiguity and evidence inconsistency.
Cross-function alignment is decisive in turning a high-pressure case into a controllable case.
uncertainties: City-level implementation and evidentiary weighting can vary.
Edge-case payment component handling may need local confirmation.
Official English texts may not fully capture all Chinese-language implementation nuances.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for scenario format because sources support both legal framing and practical multinational case interpretation.
